signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Carry + Options
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.065 — price is testing the 160.00 psychological level which previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings. Despite 287bp SOFR-TONA carry favoring USD, intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. JPY COT LF net short -115,036 (long% 29.2%) indicates a crowded carry trade vulnerable to a squeeze. Options market is pricing downside protection with a -1.10 moderate put skew (25d RR) and an elevated 3.8pt Vol Risk Premium (IV 7.2% vs HV30 3.4%).

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -22,354 (long% 23.9%) shows specs heavily fading the currency despite a neutral RBNZ bias. With NZD CESI at -4 (moderate negative bias) and the weakest labor market in ten years, RBNZ's Silk speaking today determines if the dovish shift is realized. If the labor market data forces a dovish pivot, the spec short is justified; however, any hawkish surprise risks a crowded short-unwind toward 0.5900. NZD/USD at 0.5852.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Retail Divergence + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3966 — retail sentiment is 92% short, diverging from the COT LF net short of -60,671 (long% 24.7%). This extreme retail shorting into institutional selling creates a complex reversal risk. USD/CAD vs WTI Oil correlation has broken to -0.225 (z=-1.60σ vs -0.019 norm), meaning CAD is no longer benefiting from oil price stability. 150bp Fed-BoC carry advantage supports USD upside while bears defend 1.4000.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Volatility Premium + Carry Neutrality
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3445 — options market pricing significant tail risk with a 5.0pt Vol Risk Premium (IV 11.0% vs HV30 6.0%). Fed 3.75% vs BoE 3.75% results in a 0bp carry differential, leaving the pair sensitive to data surprises. GBP COT LF net long +21,961 (long% 61.7%) aligns with UK growth resilience headlines, but the high VRP suggests traders are paying up for protection ahead of the BoE meeting on 18 Jun.
